title: Structural-reset latent tyre-state features
summary: Recent F1 tyre-state work models degradation as a latent state with pit stops acting as resets, which matches this task better than static tyre-life thresholds.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre-state work models degradation as a latent state with pit stops acting as resets, which matches this task better than static tyre-life thresholds. That can capture hidden pace loss from nonlinear wear when TyreLife and Cumulative_Degradation alone are too crude.
implementation_hint: Per Race-Year-Driver, fit a simple leakage-safe state filter on lap-time residuals using only prior laps: residualize LapTime or LapTime_Delta by race-lap medians, update a latent degradation level and slope, and hard-reset the state after PitStop=1. Feed current state, one-step forecast, and uncertainty width into the tabular model.
expected_effect: Should improve recall on nonlinear cliff laps and reduce overreliance on hand-picked compound life constants, especially late in stints.
risk: Easy to leak if the residual baseline uses future laps or full-race statistics; overfitting is possible if the state model is too flexible.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
